articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot
images.caption: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

articles.headline: Martínez: Cristiano Ronaldo sets the example for Portugal
articles.description: Roberto Martínez says Cristiano Ronaldo is not thinking about his Portugal future beyond the World Cup.

images.caption: Roberto Martínez says Cristiano Ronaldo is not thinking about his Portugal future beyond the World Cup.

article.headline: Euro hosts Germany dominate in goalless draw against Ukraine
article.description: Euro 2024 hosts Germany missed scores of chances and dominated for much of the game against Ukraine on Monday, but did not get past a 0-0 in their penultimate warmup game before the tournament start in 11 days.
article.linkText: Dominant Germany draw with Ukraine in friendly

article.story:
Euro 2024 hosts Germany missed scores of chances and dominated for much of the game against Ukraine on Monday, but did not get past a 0-0 in their penultimate warmup match before the tournament start in 11 days.

The Germans, desperate for a good Euro on home soil after a decade without any international success, hit the woodwork and came close on several occasions in a strong performance despite plenty of substitutions.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Coach Julian Nagelsmann is due to announce his final squad this week, cutting one player from his 27-man preliminary selection.

"I wish we had won 2-0 or 3-0. We played a good game over large phases. The team wanted to win and you could feel it. The first 20 minutes were very good," said Nagelsmann.

"Then the 20 minutes after the break were also very good again and we had six or seven chances to score a goal.

"Then we got caught out on some quick breaks but Manuel [Neuer] was there and he played a good game as well."

Germany, without Real Madrid and Borussia Dortmund players in the lineup following Saturday's Champions League final, but with Chancellor Olaf Scholz in the stands, started at a fast pace and with high pressing.

Ilkay Gündogan should have put them in the lead in the 16th minute when Pascal Gross sent a cross to the far post for a header, but the unmarked Germany captain failed to connect properly.

The Ukrainians, who qualified for the Euros following a playoff win over Iceland, left possession to their opponents and instead tried to counter-attack using Mykhailo Mudryk's speed.

Germany, who play their final warmup game against Greece in four days, came close again early in the second half but Kai Havertz's glancing header sailed wide in the 53rd.

Substitute Maximilian Beier, making his national team debut, came even closer with his first touch, hitting the crossbar from a tight angle and then forcing a save from goalkeeper Anatoliy Trubin minutes later.

Trubin also denied Chris Fuehrich from the edge of the box a little later.

The visitors got a bigger share of possession after an hour and could have snatched the win in the last minute but Germany keeper Neuer, making his first national team appearance since the 2022 World Cup, stopped Andriy Yarmolenko's effort from a tight angle.

Germany open the tournament with their Group A match against Scotland on June 14. They also play Hungary and Switzerland in their group.

Ukraine will start their tournament on June 17 against Romania in Group E and will also take on Slovakia and Belgium.
